evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Define and govern event taxonomy; Build and maintain dimensional models; Create foundational tables; Monitor data quality, latency, and completeness; data contracts; metrics/semantical layers
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour: governance, dimensional modeling, data contracts, monitoring, and completeness checks dominate; no velocity language.
evidence.domain_risk: marketing analytics; channel performance; attribution; errors affect marketing ROI without regulatory/financial consequence
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Moderate: SaaS/marketing domain; decisions affect revenue efficiency but without regulatory/financial reporting risk.
evidence.data_team_maturity: own the data foundation; design event tracking; build data models; establish semantic layers
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Early: candidate owns foundation-building for marketing analytics; establishing tracking schemas and data models suggests greenfield or early-stage analytics.
evidence.jd_authorship: Define and govern event taxonomy for marketing touchpoints; Build and maintain dimensional models; Create foundational tables; Manage ingestion pipelines; Work closely with Marketing Analyst
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Hiring manager: specific ownership of event taxonomy, dimensional modeling, foundational table design, and pipeline management shows domain expertise.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: attribution, LTV, and channel performance analysis; campaign tracking; marketing touchpoints; ad platforms; marketing analytics
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Commercial: primary focus on marketing ROI, attribution, channel efficiency, and customer acquisition metrics.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own the data foundation; Define and govern event taxonomy; Build and maintain models; Create foundational tables; you'll design event tracking
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic: candidate sets direction for marketing analytics foundation and owns establishment of taxonomy and models.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools or expected AI capability; tech stack mentions LLM providers in company context, not role requirement.
evidence.testing_framing: data contracts; Monitor data quality, latency, and completeness; Hands-on experience with data transformation tools with testing
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Responsibility: data quality monitoring and data contracts owned as core responsibilities.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Define and govern event taxonomy; Monitor data quality, latency, and completeness across the marketing data pipeline; Validate tracking
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: data quality and tracking validation concern secondary to analytics enablement and model delivery.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: own the data foundation; design event tracking; build data models; establish foundational tables; manage ingestion pipelines
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mixed: building foundation for marketing analytics (greenfield tracking/models) while maintaining and optimizing pipelines.
evidence.loss_aversion: Define and govern event taxonomy; Monitor data quality, latency, and completeness; Validate tracking across channels.
